name: Formula One 1967
attribution: Historical data derived from f1db (github.com/f1db/f1db, CC BY 4.0)
AI ratings from 'Custom AI by jusk - F1 1967 Season' (F-Vintage_Gen1.xml), matching Alain Fry's F1 1967 skinpack
notes: Coverage-note scope: each 'entrant ... not included' line below refers to that exact f1db constructor/engine/round stint, not necessarily to the named driver. The max-grid roster may represent the driver through an available skinpack livery proxy while still omitting the exact historical car combination.

Per-track AI overrides from the source custom-AI XML (tracks="..." entries) are carried into the matching rounds' aiOverrides as absolute partial ratings patches. Entries authored against layouts this calendar does not drive (Azure_Circuit_2021, Watkins_Glen_S, Spa_Francorchamps_1993, Nordschleife_2020/_24hr) are mapped to the calendar round at the same venue (Monaco, Watkins Glen, Spa, Nurburgring).

v1.2.0: jusk's F-Vintage_Gen1.xml is the ratings SOURCE OF TRUTH for its 20 named livery drivers. All 13 base rating dimensions for those drivers are taken from the XML's base blocks (superseding the earlier f1db-derived raceSkill/qualifyingSkill, which had narrowed the field's spread); the eight later max-grid substitutes retain their f1db-derived raceSkill/qualifyingSkill and deliberately leave unauthored traits unset. Per-track source blocks are carried into matching rounds as absolute partial patches, filtered to starters. The earlier f1db-derived per-round driverForm overlay was removed in favour of jusk's hand-tuned per-track form. Rebuilt via tools/import_jusk_ai.cs.

v1.3.0: all 20 vehicle_reliability values from jusk's v0.3 F-Vintage_Gen1.xml are carried as staged per-driver car data. Historical substitute drivers inherit the value attached to their shared skinpack livery; Jo Bonnier's round-11 switch from the Rodriguez livery to the Rindt livery is represented by a round override. Scarfiotti's source-authored Monza rating patch is restored now that the max-grid roster includes him there. These staged values affect new careers only; the deterministic career sim continues to use team reliability.
